panels.title: Browser traces (fleet-dashboard-web)
panels.description: Headline panel: recent service.name=fleet-dashboard-web traces from Tempo, emitted by the React SPA's @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-web SDK. This is the exact tag the phase proof searches Tempo for; it populates once a real browser loads the SPA at http://localhost:8080 and the BatchSpanProcessor flushes spans OTLP/HTTP to Alloy. Root traces are 'documentLoad' (page load) and 'HTTP GET' (the REST snapshot fetch/XHR calls). Click a Trace ID to drill into the spans.

panels.title: Document load
panels.description: Isolates the page-load span the DocumentLoadInstrumentation emits: the 'documentLoad' root span (with its documentFetch and resourceFetch child timings) under service.name=fleet-dashboard-web. Surfaces SPA load latency — how long the browser took to fetch and parse the dashboard document and its static resources. Click a Trace ID to inspect the documentFetch / resourceFetch waterfall.

panels.title: REST snapshot fetches
panels.description: The fetch/XHR client spans for the SPA's one-time REST snapshot reads (GET /vehicles, GET /vehicles/anomalies/latest, GET /zones/counts), emitted by the Fetch/XMLHttpRequest instrumentations under service.name=fleet-dashboard-web. The SDK names these client spans 'HTTP GET'; each carries http.url / http.status_code attributes and injects a W3C traceparent onto the cross-origin frontend-api request. tableType=spans lists one row per fetch span with its http.url.

panels.title: Browser → frontend-api joined trace
panels.description: Distributed-trace proof of the traceparent join: traces that contain BOTH a service.name=fleet-dashboard-web spanset AND a service.name=frontend-api spanset. The browser's 'HTTP GET' CLIENT span propagates a W3C traceparent onto the cross-origin REST fetch, so frontend-api parents its server span (e.g. 'GET /vehicles/anomalies/latest') under the same trace. Click a Trace ID to drill into the browser → frontend-api waterfall spanning both services.
